title: French economic growth undercut by war in Iran, national statistics office says
description: The French economy will grow by only 0.2% in the first half of the year instead of the 0.3% forecast, the Insee national statistics agency said Tuesday, citing higher inflation linked to the war in Iran. Inflation will surpass 2% in coming months, rising sharply from the 1.1% rate seen in February.

description:

title: Tension rises between Israel and Lebanon as IDF occupies south of Litani river
description: Israel shows no signs of slowing down its military campaign in Lebanon. It has so far killed at least 1039 people and displaced more than a million in more than three weeks of fighting with the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah. Instead,Tel Aviv said on March 24 its military will seize control of the area south of the Litani river. Hezbollah has vowed to fight back.

title: Iran strikes put in question US-Israeli attacks's impact on military capabilities
description: Iranian drone strikes battered Tel Aviv, damaging apartments and injuring several people. The attacks put into question whether Iran's military capabilities have truly been weakened by US-Israeli attacks. It has been launching sporadic strikes to preserve its arsenal while letting its enemies' stockpile run out.

title: 'I intend to go back to my country': Exiled Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine
description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine said he intends to return to his country, days after resurfacing in Washington following nearly two months in hiding in Uganda. "I am still under threat," Wine warned, calling for international sanctions against Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni and his son.

title: Analysis: Naim Qassem says Hezbollah will continue fighting Israel, frames war as defense of Lebanon
description:
Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem gave his second speech regarding the renewed war with Israel, once again arguing that his group was the victim of aggression and not the aggressor. He said that Hezbollah is fighting a war of self-defense on behalf of Lebanon and stressed that it would continue fighting until it secures adequate concessions from Israel.

description:

title: Israeli strikes eliminate Iranian security chief Larijani and Basij commander Soleimani
description:
Overnight Israeli strikes in Iran killed Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani. The eliminations add to the growing list of top Iranian officials who have been killed by Israeli and American strikes since the conflict began on February 28.

title: Trump's supposed talks with Iran: What do we know?
description: US President Donald Trump on Monday walked back his own deadline for escalating attacks on Iran, citing "productive" talks with Tehran – but Iranian officials deny those talks took place. So what is really happening? Here is a look at the facts and speculation.

title: Middle East war weighs heavy on Iranian population
description: Getting a sense of the real death toll and the psychological toll of the war in the Middle East is a real challenge. According to estimates, at least 1,500 Iranians have died since the start of the US-Israeli military campaign. We spoke to a sociologist on how it is effecting civilians. Please be warned some of these images are disturbing.

title: EU and Australia sign free trade deal and boost defence cooperation
description: The European Union and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on March 24. The two countries have also agreed to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. Speaking while visiting Australia, EU chief Ursula von der Leyen said it was necessary at a time where tariffs and supplu chains are being weaponised.

title: US 'shifts' towards diplomacy with Iran ahead of midterms
description: Hopes rose that the Iran war could begin winding down as U.S. President Donald Trump said that his government was holding productive talks with Tehran. Although this was denied by Iran, it is a further sign that the US is turning towards diplomacy to end a conflict that could drag on. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle breaks it down for us.

title: Israeli forces to control south Lebanon up to Litani river
description: Israel said on March 24 that its military would take control of south Lebanon up to the Litani River, around 30 kilometres (20 miles) from the border, as deadly strikes pounded the country. The day before, Israel's finance minister had said Israel should annex the region. This comes as Israel continues to target Hezbollah targets in south Lebanon and southern suburbs of Beirut. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin breaks it down for us.

title: What do we know about the negotiations between the US and Iran?
description: It's day 25 of the US and Israel's war with Iran, and US President Donald Trump claims that "very good" talks are underway to end combat. He said that because of this, the US will hold off on striking Iranian power plants. But Iranian officials have contradicted this - downplaying Trump's threats and saying he's actually backing down.

title: In US, thousands of Cuban migrants who arrived legally fear deportation
description: Cubans, who have historically received preferential treatment from the US, have not been spared by President Donald Trump's war on immigration. The US has ended family reunification and legal immigration from Cuba by adding the island to its travel ban. It has also suspended temporary protection programmes, exposing thousands of people who arrived legally to deportation to a country suffering from severe US sanctions and crippling electricity bla

title: Afghanistan's Taliban government releases detained US national Dennis Coyle
description: Afghanistan’s Taliban government said on Tuesday it had released American academic Dennis Coyle after more than a year in detention. Coyle was arrested in January 2025 on allegations he violated laws, although Afghan authorities never publicly stated what laws he was accused of violating.

title: Nigeria's Dangote refinery increases exports as Middle East war shakes global markets
description: Nigeria's Dangote Petroleum Refinery has stepped up gasoline exports across Africa as disruptions to energy flows due to the Iran conflict squeeze traditional ​fuel ‌supply routes, curbing the cheap imports that long dominated West African ⁠markets. As the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, the refinery could also see a surge in European demand, especially for jet fuel. FRANCE 24's Clemence Waller has more.

title: French judge probes ex-EU border chief for complicity in crimes against humanity
description: A French judge is set to investigate former Frontex chief Fabrice Leggeri, now a member of the European Parliament for Marine Le Pen's far-right National Rally, over allegations he encouraged staff to facilitate the interception of migrant boats in the Mediterranean Sea.

title: 'Paris started with an openness to queer people': Author Rasheed Newson
description: A leading author is speaking in Paris about the French capital's ability to draw gay and bisexual people to it. Rasheed Newson's talk is entitled "From Baldwin to Today: Queer Black Expats in Paris". He says that many queer African Americans seeking freedom and creative possibility have come to Paris due to its openness to queer people. Newson is the author of the bestseller "My Government Means to Kill Me", and has a new book is coming out short

title: Thousands flock to see Greece’s annual 'pink veil' of peach blossoms
description: Visitors from across Greece but increasingly from across Europe as well are flocking to see the blossoming peach trees in the Veria region, located roughly 72km west of the country's second city Thessaloniki. The growing popularity of the annual spring celebrations organised around the blooms is proving to be a boon for local farmers and the tourism industry.

title: Shabaab celebrates October attack on Mogadishu prison in new video
description:
Shabaab, Al Qaeda’s branch in East Africa, highlighted last year’s brazen assault on Mogadishu’s Godka Jilacow prison, operated by Somalia’s National Intelligence and Security Agency. In the video, one of the attackers was identified as the son of Ali Mohamed Rage, the group’s top spokesman.

Iranian-backed militias have continued conducting attacks across Iraq, recently targeting US and foreign diplomatic sites in Baghdad and Erbil. Hundreds of strikes have hit the Kurdistan Region since late February, wounding Peshmerga forces and damaging key locations. While Kataib Hezbollah signaled a possible temporary pause in attacks tied to specific demands on March 18, other groups, such as Kataib Sayyid al Shuhada, vowed to continue.

title: Analysis: West Bloomfield terror attack highlights Hezbollah connections within Shiite Lebanese community in US
description:
Last week, Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, a Lebanese-US dual national, rammed his car into the entrance of Temple Israel, a synagogue in West Bloomfield, Indiana. Ghazali had familial connections to Hezbollah, and evidence shows multiple connections to the US-designated terrorist group among Shiite Lebanese expatriates, including open support for Hezbollah by some individuals within these communities.

title: Tehran calls Trump's talk claims 'fake news' to reassure markets

Driven by record concentrations of greenhouse gases, climate change is intensifying across interconnected systems: oceans, cryosphere, and atmosphere. title: Increase in hostilities between israel and Hezbollah shows 'no sign of slowing down'
description: An Israeli strike on Bshamoun, a town south of Beirut, killed at least two people. It also continued strikes on the southern suburbs of the capital. Since the start of Israeli strikes on the country, at least 1,039 people have been killed. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin is in Beirut, and tells us more from the ground.

title: EU, Australia seal free trade deal eight years in the making
description: The EU and Australia have signed a sweeping free trade agreement that will eliminate tariffs on nearly all trade between them as global economies seek to hedge against chaotic US tariff policies. Also in this edition: crude oil prices rise again as traders remain sceptical of signals that Washington is trying to de-escalate its war with Iran.

title: ICE agents start appearing in US airports amid shutdown chaos
description: The Trump administration has begun deploying ICE agents across several airports in the US. Officials say the agents are being sent to at least 14 airports - including hubs in New York, Chicago and Atlanta - to assist overstretched TSA staff during a government shutdown that has caused long lines at security checkpoints across the country.

title: EU signs trade deal with Australia as it looks for new partners amid tension with US
description: The European Union and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on March 24, while also agreeing to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. It's the latest Brussels has agreed to, to diversify trade. But the deal was slammed by EU farmers, who have called 'unacceptable'. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating has more from Brussels.

title: Unclear if Trump is telling truth about talks with Iran or if it's 'bluff'
description: US President Donald Trump has said that the US and Iran are having 'very good' talks regarding the war. But they were denied by Tehran, although they did say they had received a request from the White House. There is also the question of where talks would be held - with Pakistan coming out as the most likely location. But the major question is: 'is Trump telling the truth about points of agreement, or is it bluff?' asks FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorj

title: For Israel, war against Iran is not over as Trump's attempt to reach deal
description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israel would be ready to protect its vital interests under any circumstances, while it continues to strike Iran and Lebanon. This comes as US President Donald Trump has claimed talks were underway with Iran - despite Tehran denying those talks. 'The question is if the US pull out of the war, can Israel carry on with it', says FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani.

title: Misleading campaign adverts target French far left in local elections

It happened in the middle of France’s electoral silence period, raising suspicions of foreign interference. Emerald Maxwell explains in this edition of Truth or Fake.

title: Video Shows Saab 340 Airborne Radar Flying in Ukraine
description: The Saab 340 AEW, also called the S 100D Argus, will enhance Ukraine’s situational awareness when facing Russian cruise missile and kamikaze drone barrages. Ukraine appears to have received its first Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEWC) aircraft, after the Swedish Saab 340 airborne radar was spotted flying in an unofficial video that emerged on []

title: Analysis: Operational gains against Iran continue, but threats to US forces persist
description:
The United States, along with its Israeli partners, continues to make considerable progress toward its military objectives of removing Iran’s primary military means to threaten the region in the third week of Operation Epic Fury. Despite these operational successes, however, Tehran and its proxies retain the ability to attack US forces.

title: Eritrea to end 18-year AFCON isolation by playing Eswatini in qualifier
description: Eritrea vs Eswatini on Wednesday will serve as the first AFCON qualifier for the east African nation since 2008.

title: Where do reported US-Iran ‘negotiations’ leave Israel?
description: Israeli analysts describe confusion as US President Trump unexpectedly shifts from threatened strikes on Iran to talks.

title: Palestine weekly wrap: West Bank attacks surge, Israel restricts Gaza aid
description: Settler attacks and land seizures marked a week that was supposed to be one of celebration for Palestinians.

title: Top EU official calls for negotiations with Iran and an end to war
description: European Commission President Ursula Von der Leyen has called for negotiations with Iran and an end to hostilities.

title: Europe Clinches Critical Mineral Access With Australian Trade Deal
description: The European Union and Australia have shaken hands on a trade deal that would improve access to aluminum, lithium and other critical minerals for the 27-nation bloc.

title: Families uprooted from the Gaza border now find themselves in Iranian crosshairs
description:

title: 6 people lightly hurt as Tel Aviv hit in Iranian missile attack
description:

title: Live: Iran launches missiles on Israel, calls Trump’s negotiation claims ‘fake news'
description: President Donald Trump started the fourth week of his war against Iran by offering the world some guarded optimism that the US could soon be winding operations down, but Iran contradicted his claims of progress being made in diplomatic talks. Iran also launched another round of missiles towards Israel early Tuesday, state television announced. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.

title: China dials back on fuel price hikes to 'reduce burden' on drivers
description: It comes as countries across the region are taking various measures to weather the soaring cost of fuel.

title: ICE agents deployed to US airports: Which airports are affected?
description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers were reportedly sent to 14 airports, including New York’s JFK.

title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 25 of attacks?
description: Trump claims talks on with Iran, as he holds off on energy attacks, but Tehran denies any negotiations.

title: More Australian beef headed for Europe under new EU trade deal
